papers.title: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown strong potential for end-to-end autonomous driving by jointly modeling visual perception, language reasoning, explainability and action prediction. However, their large vision-language backbones and reasoning modules introduce substantial inference latency and thereby prevent their deployment in the unforgiving reality of the road networks. We propose RT-VLA, a lightweight, distilled VLA model that transfers the driving and reasoning capabilities of the state-of-the-art SimLingo model into a compact student through multi-level supervised distillation. RT-VLA preserves language-based reasoning and supports post-hoc explanation through offline language analysis of safety-critical driving moments without adding latency to real-time control. Compared to the SimLingo teacher, RT-VLA maintains competitive closed-loop driving and language reasoning performance while reducing inference time by 44.8X in vision-only mode and 7.9X in vision+language mode. These results suggest that supervised distillation is a practical approach for building real-time, explainable VLA-style autonomous driving models.

papers.title: An integrated interpretable control effectiveness learning and nonlinear control allocation methodology for overactuated aircrafts
papers.abstract: Nonlinear dynamics and the strong couplings that arise between multiple effectors undermine the assumptions behind conventional, linear control allocation techniques. When flight enters regimes where nonlinear effects dominate, linear allocators exhibit reduced accuracy due to increased model mismatch, which subsequently degrades performance and robustness of the flight control system. High fidelity onboard models and black box data driven approaches can recover accuracy across the flight envelope, but respectively impose computational burdens prohibitive for real time allocation and sacrifice the interpretability required for verification and fault diagnosis. This paper addresses these limitations by learning an explicit, physics constrained analytical model of the control effectiveness mapping from representative flight data using Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics. The resulting mapping is compact, interpretable, and admits analytical derivatives, enabling efficient computation within nonlinear solvers that additionally incorporate actuator dynamics, without requiring an onboard model. An online adaptation mechanism monitors prediction residuals and refreshes the model when significant plant changes are detected, providing graceful reconfiguration under actuator failures and varying operating conditions. The methodology is evaluated on a high fidelity nonlinear benchmark aircraft across a range of aggressive maneuvers, achieving accuracy comparable to a full nonlinear onboard model while substantially reducing computational cost relative to established baselines.

executive_summary: New control-theoretic and optimization tools push motion planning and MPC forward, from semidefinite relaxations for collision-free planning to robust MPC for deformable objects.
Vision-Language-Action (VLA) research continues to mature, with multiple works on real-time execution, robustness, physics grounding, and fine-tuning stability for manipulation and driving.
Navigation advances span floorplan-guided embodied navigation, reliability-aware and agentic UAV planners, multi-agent lifelong exploration, and BIM- and Gaussian-based mapping/localization.
Dexterous and contact-rich manipulation receive significant infrastructure upgrades, including open-source hand platforms, exoskeleton and haptic interfaces, and synergy-inspired hand hardware.
Safety and reliability are central themes across domains, with new frameworks for provably safe RL, safe highway driving RL, SOTIF-style safety envelopes, and auditing/repairing spurious policies.
Teleoperation and data-collection pipelines evolve with richer haptics, egocentric guidance, and robot-free interfaces, directly supporting scalable learning for mobile and whole-body manipulation.

institution_matches.title: Semidefinite Relaxations for Collision-Free Motion Planning
institution_matches.overview: Formulates point-robot motion planning with spherical obstacles as a polynomial-curve optimization and analyzes a semidefinite relaxation that is provably equivalent to a related higher-dimensional planning problem. The symmetry-reduced relaxation scales linearly with polynomial degree, is dimension-independent, and runs 10–100× faster than SNOPT/IPOPT while serving effectively as a convex steering function in RRT for high-order quadrotor trajectory planning.

institution_matches.title: The N2D Haptic Glove: A Multi-Finger Glove for 2D Directional Force Feedback for Contact Rich Manipulation
institution_matches.overview: Introduces a multi-finger haptic glove that renders planar fingertip forces via capstan transmissions, providing high-transparency, 2D directional feedback during manipulation. Bench tests and teleoperation user studies show reduced contact-force error, improved consistency, and better user experience versus visual-only and single-axis haptics, supporting more precise contact-rich teleoperation and learning from demonstration.

topic_matches.title: Whole-Body Impedance Model Predictive Control for Safe Physical Human--Robot Interaction on Floating-Base Platforms
topic_matches.overview: Presents a three-level control architecture for floating-base robots that combines centroidal MPC, priority-based whole-body control, and a null-space impedance MPC with disturbance estimation. The approach guarantees zero steady-state error under bounded constant physical interaction loads and runs at ≥1 kHz via contact-consistent feedback linearization and offline QP precomputation, enabling safe, compliant pHRI during locomotion.

topic_matches.title: Impedance MPC with Disturbance Estimation for Dexterous Hand Control
topic_matches.overview: Develops an actuator-agnostic Impedance MPC framework that reduces tendon-driven fingers to constant-coefficient double integrators, enabling 500 Hz constrained MPC with encoder-only disturbance estimation. On a hydraulic finger and a 16-DOF simulated hand, it enforces contact and actuation limits while dramatically improving tracking accuracy and stiffness control over classical impedance in contact-rich tasks.

topic_matches.title: ORCA: A Platform for Open-Source Dexterity Research
topic_matches.overview: Introduces the ORCA stack, an open-source ecosystem that unifies anthropomorphic hand hardware control, simulation, teleoperation, and retargeting behind a single interface tightly integrated with robot-learning frameworks like LeRobot. Demonstrations show end-to-end pipelines from VR-based teleoperation data collection to learned in-hand reorientation policies, lowering barriers for reproducible dexterous manipulation research.

topic_matches.title: Spatially Conditioned Diffusion Policy: Learning Precise and Robust Manipulation with a Single RGB Camera
topic_matches.overview: Proposes SCDP, a diffusion-based visuomotor policy that uses multi-scale visual features and spatial conditioning along end-effector trajectories to focus attention in single-camera setups. The method matches or surpasses multi-camera baselines in simulation and shows precise, distraction-robust manipulation on real robots without wrist-mounted views.

topic_matches.title: ForestBack: Breadcrumb-Based Pedestrian Dead Reckoning for Infrastructure-Free Return Navigation
topic_matches.overview: Introduces ForestBack, an infrastructure-free return-navigation system that records reversible breadcrumb nodes using IMU, magnetometer, and barometric data to reconstruct and invert walking paths. On indoor obstacle-avoidance routes it cuts RMSE and final-position error by ~16–22% over standard PDR while achieving near-perfect turn-event detection, providing a lightweight navigation aid in GPS-denied settings.

topic_matches.title: FloVerse: Floor Plan-Guided Multi-Modal Navigation
topic_matches.overview: Defines FloVerse, a unified task that extends floorplan-guided navigation to PointNav, ObjectNav, and ImageNav, and introduces a 1.6K-scene dataset with 240K trajectories. The ThreeDiff policy combines a planner, a multimodal diffusion goal reasoner, and a depth-based refiner, showing that floorplan priors significantly boost navigation performance and can be implicitly exploited via diffusion-based models.

topic_matches.title: BIM-Loc: BIM-Integrated Discrepancy-Aware LiDAR-based Indoor Localization
topic_matches.overview: Presents BIM-Loc, which localizes robots indoors by directly registering LiDAR scans to Building Information Models while jointly estimating discrepancies between as-designed and as-built structures. A multi-hit ray-casting data association, BIM-integrated pose-graph optimization, and hierarchical Bayesian discrepancy detection yield higher localization accuracy and robustness than existing map-based methods.

topic_matches.title: Universal Manipulation Exoskeleton: Learning Compliant Whole-body Policies with Real-time Torque Feedback
topic_matches.overview: Introduces UME, a low-cost upper-limb exoskeleton that records joint torques and poses while providing real-time haptic feedback for high-fidelity teleoperation across multiple robot arms. The system enables learning of bimanual and whole-body compliant policies that succeed in long-horizon mobile manipulation, force-mediated tasks, and highly constrained environments, including visually occluded scenarios.

topic_matches.title: Robustness without Wrinkles: Parallel Simulation and Robust MPC for Certified Deformable Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: CORD-SLS combines a GPU-parallel differentiable simulator with contact smoothing and an output-feedback robust MPC scheme to control ropes and cloth under uncertainty. Conformal prediction calibrates perception-error bounds, yielding safe reachable tubes; experiments in rope routing and cloth folding show millisecond-level planning, higher safety, and better task success than prior methods.

topic_matches.title: Guided Diffusion with Distilled Vision-Language Reliability for Aerial Navigation
topic_matches.overview: Proposes a diffusion-based UAV trajectory planner that conditions on both observations and a reliability heatmap distilled from a vision-language model to flag untrustworthy regions like glass or glare. A differentiable ESDF-based guidance treats unreliable areas as virtual obstacles, cutting obstacle violations from ~40% to ~10% and increasing average reliability of traversed regions, while running up to 2× faster than using the full VLM online.
